channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - AI & Machine Learning
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: AI & Machine Learning

item.title: Development and validation of a machine learning model and a web tool for predicting the risk of Crohn's disease diagnosis in Chinese patients.
item.description: Public health - Diagnostic delay in Crohn's disease (CD) may increase the risk of poor prognosis. This study developed and validated a machine learning (ML) model for clinicians to conduct preliminary diagnosis an...

item.title: Intestinal alkaline phosphatase and gut health: insights into homeostasis, barrier protection, and immune signaling.
item.description: Food & function - Review - Intestinal alkaline phosphatase (IAP) is a brush border enzyme critical for maintaining gut homeostasis by detoxifying bacterial endotoxins, regulating nutrient metabolism, and modulating immune re...

item.title: Astragalin alleviates ulcerative colitis via FPR1 inhibition and restores Microbiota-Metabolite Homeostasis: A mechanism revealed by deep learning.
item.description: Biochemical pharmacology - The pursuit of multi-targeted therapies that simultaneously address mucosal immune dysregulation, barrier dysfunction and gut microbiota imbalance in ulcerative colitis (UC) remains a major challen...

item.title: Automated Eosinophil Quantification Using Deep Learning to Predict Therapy Escalation in Pediatric Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Clinical and translational gastroenterology - Emerging evidence implicates eosinophils as important modulators of disease activity and therapeutic response in ulcerative colitis. Automated image analysis provides a scalable and reproducible ap...

item.title: Phage Therapy in Gastrointestinal Diseases: Current Status and Challenges.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Review - A phage is a virus that targets bacteria with high precision. While phage therapy provides a targeted alternative to broad-spectrum antibiotics, it is not completely free from the challenges of ant...

item.title: Neurotransmitter-mediated gut-brain axis: a bibliometric analysis of research trends and knowledge structure.
item.description: Frontiers in microbiology - Systematic review - The gut-brain axis constitutes a bidirectional network linking the gastrointestinal tract and central nervous system through neural, endocrine, metabolic, and immune pathways. Neurotransmitters pla...

item.title: Non-invasive assessment of inflammatory bowel disease activity using a DIA-derived stool peptidomic signature and machine learning.
item.description: Frontiers in molecular biosciences - Monitoring disease activity in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is essential for guiding therapy and preventing irreversible tissue damage. Colonoscopy, although the gold standard, is invasive and ...
